objectives: Identify core blockchain components and how they interact
Explain blockchain architecture at a high level
Describe blockchain openness and its implications for applications
learning_path.title: Blockchain Foundations: Components, Architecture, and Openness

objectives: Define what blockchain applications are and how they differ from traditional applications
Map common blockchain application areas to suitable design choices
Evaluate when blockchain is appropriate for a given problem
learning_path.title: Blockchain Applications and Application Areas

objectives: Explain hashing, signatures, and Merkle trees in blockchain contexts
Connect cryptographic primitives to integrity and authenticity guarantees
Recognize common cryptographic misuse patterns in blockchain applications
learning_path.title: Cryptography Essentials for Blockchains

objectives: Describe how the Ethereum Virtual Machine executes smart contracts
Explain state, transactions, and gas at a conceptual level
Relate EVM behavior to smart contract design constraints
learning_path.title: Ethereum Execution Model: EVM Fundamentals

objectives: Write basic Solidity contracts with functions and state variables
Use common Solidity patterns safely at an introductory level
Understand compilation targets in relation to the EVM
learning_path.title: Solidity Fundamentals for Smart Contract Development

objectives: Set up and use a smart contract development framework workflow
Compile, test, and deploy contracts in a local development environment
Structure projects for maintainability and repeatable builds
learning_path.title: Smart Contract Development Tooling

objectives: Use Node.js to run scripts that support blockchain development workflows
Organize a simple Node.js project for interacting with blockchain systems
Handle configuration and environment setup for development tasks
learning_path.title: Node.js Foundations for Blockchain Development

objectives: Connect to an Ethereum-compatible network from an application
Read contract state and send transactions from a client script
Handle common interaction patterns and errors
learning_path.title: Interacting with Smart Contracts Using Web3 Libraries

objectives: Differentiate ERC-20, ERC-721, and ERC-1155 use cases
Implement or integrate token-standard interfaces in Solidity
Understand standard-driven behaviors relevant to applications
learning_path.title: Token Standards: ERC-20, ERC-721, and ERC-1155

objectives: Apply blockchain applications security principles to common designs
Identify threat models specific to blockchain applications
Prioritize mitigations based on impact and likelihood
learning_path.title: Blockchain Application Security Principles

objectives: Recognize common smart contract vulnerability classes
Apply secure design and coding practices in Solidity
Validate fixes through targeted testing and review
learning_path.title: Smart Contract Security: Vulnerabilities and Defenses

objectives: Explain the Solana program model at a high level for development
Build a basic Solana program using Anchor
Understand how account constraints influence program design
learning_path.title: Solana Smart Contracts with Anchor (Rust)

matched_occupation.description: Blockchain developers implement or program blockchain-based software systems based on specifications and designs by using programming languages, tools, and blockchain platforms.
matched_occupation.label: blockchain developer
